steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side
steps.content: The left side ends with 'crowd-monitoring,' a modifier awaiting its head noun. The right side contains 'video streamed from cameras in heavily touristed areas,' a noun phrase, followed by 'the AI algorithm determines crowd sizes...,' which is an independent clause. These two elements must be properly separated.

steps.title: Apply the rule for sentence boundaries and introductory phrases
steps.content:
A period after 'system' (choice C) allows 'Analyzing video streamed from cameras in heavily touristed areas' to function as an introductory participial phrase in a new sentence, properly separated by a comma from the main clause 'the AI algorithm determines crowd sizes...' Choices A (nested em-dashes), B, and D all place 'the AI algorithm determines' in the same sentence without proper separation, creating comma splices.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
